title: Population Growth Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive MicroSim modeling exponential and logistic population growth with adjustable parameters for initial population, growth rate, and carrying capacity.

learningObjectives: Model exponential and logistic population growth by adjusting parameters and observing resulting curves
Explain the relationship between growth rate (r), carrying capacity (K), and population size (N)
Predict how changes in environmental conditions affect population growth patterns
Compare exponential and logistic growth models and identify when each applies
prerequisites: Basic understanding of population ecology vocabulary
Understanding of exponential functions from algebra

keyQuestions: Why does the logistic growth curve level off while the exponential curve does not?
At what population size is the growth rate fastest in logistic growth?
What real-world factors determine a population's carrying capacity?
Can any population grow exponentially forever?
commonMisconceptions: Populations always grow exponentially when resources are available
Carrying capacity is a fixed number that never changes
Population growth stops completely at carrying capacity rather than fluctuating around it
assessmentOpportunities: Students predict population trajectories before running the simulation and compare to actual results
Students explain the biological meaning of the inflection point at K/2
Students identify real-world scenarios that match exponential vs. logistic growth
